description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that builds Linux packages using distinct Docker images: debian:bullseye for build_debian_package, ubuntu:rolling for build_ubuntu_package, ubuntu:latest for build_no_apparmor, fedora:latest for build_fedora_package, almalinux:latest for build_redhat_package, and the custom image registry.salsa.debian.org/salsa-ci-team/ci-image-git-buildpackage:latest for debian_ci. Each job runs a 10‑minute timeout, updates the package manager (apt‑get or dnf), installs build-essential, fakeroot, lintian, libapparmor-dev, pkg-config, gawk (or rpm‑build, gcc, make), prints environment variables, executes ./configure (failing with config.log on error), runs make dist, builds the package with ./mkdeb.sh or ./platform/rpm/mkrpm.sh, installs the resulting .deb or .rpm, and runs make installcheck (additionally checking that AppArmor support is disabled in build_no_apparmor). The Debian and Ubuntu jobs set DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive. The debian_ci job checks out the current commit, enables sid and experimental APT sources, downloads the source package, extracts it, updates the changelog with dch, creates an orig tarball, commits changes, and finally runs ./ci/printenv.sh, gitlab-ci-git-buildpackage and gitlab-ci-lintian. All jobs run in the default stage without explicit caches or artifacts.
